content.full_text:
CITIZENS FOR FAIR RENT CONTROL  
1678 Shattuck Ave., Box 273  
Berkeley, CA 94709

August 3, 1990

Fair Political Practices Campaign  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento CA 95804  
Attn: Peggy Bernardo

Re: Fund raising for multiple candidates

Dear Ms Bernardo:

This is a follow-up request for written confirmation of the information you gave me during our telephone conversation August 1 (as noted in the reference above).

My understanding is that the Citizens for Fair Rent Control (CFRC) may solicit contributions for multiple candidates. The money must be divided equally between each candidate and be deposited into each candidate’s individual bank account. Pro-rata disbursements are made from each of these accounts.

In reality this appears as if there is a great deal of money juggling going on. For example, CFRC receives checks on one day for a total of $1000. CFRC writes checks for each candidate (5) in the amount of $250 which they deposit in their account. Then, the CFRC prepares a mailer for all five candidates and gives each candidate a bill for their pro-rata share.

CFRC prepares a mailer which features each of the five candidates. The printer’s charge is $600 and the mailing charge is $200 for total cost is $800. Each candidate’s share is $200. Each candidate then writes a check for $200 and gives it to the CFRC. CFRC deposits the 5 checks into the CFRC account and then writes one check to satisfy the printer and another to the post office. All of these transactions are obviously reported.

So, for the $1000 contribution:
1. CFRC deposits the money into the CFRC bank account, writes five $200 checks, and ultimately includes the credits and debits in the accounting statements.
2. Each candidate deposits a $200 check from CFRC, writes checks for expenditures to someone (including CFRC), and ultimately includes the the credits and debits in the accounting statement.
3. If the checks go to CFRC, then CFRC again makes a deposit (of money already deposited once), pays the bills and ultimately accounts for false (or duplicate) income and the expenditures.

Therefore, the $1000, or portions of it, theoretically can (and probably will) be accounted for as a credit and a debit in up to six bank accounts. The methodology is

The methodology is Machiavellian, and furthermore, it appears that the "Good Guys" raised double the money they actually did. (This is not the image we strive for in this community!)

It would be much more accurate if the money were collected by CFRC and deposited into CFRC's bank account. Then, in the accounting procedures the money collected by CFRC is divided equally among each candidate, dispersed pro-rata for each candidate. The accounting reports, completed by the CFRC treasurer, show the pro-rata money credited to each candidate, and the pro-rata expenditures debited from each candidate. The money is then accounted for only ONE time.

At any rate, please advise in writing of correct procedures. Thank you for your help on these tricky ones!

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Jeannine Davis-Kimball, Ph.D.
Campaign Manager
Citizens for Fair Rent Control
